Subject: Preliminary Cybersecurity Incident Report — Non-public; SET disclosure pending; PDPA impact assessment in progress
Issuer: AzuraTech Holdings Public Company Limited (AzuraTech PCL), a company listed on the Stock Exchange of Thailand; contact: investor.relations@azuratech.co.th
On 2 June 2026 at 03:40 ICT, our Security Operations Center detected unauthorized access to an on-prem HR file server and a SaaS code repository used by our Bangkok and Chonburi operations; immediate containment was executed by isolating affected subnets and revoking compromised API keys.
Preliminary scope indicates potential exposure of personal data as defined under the PDPA, including employee full names, internal email addresses (e.g., firstname.lastname@azuratech.co.th), mobile numbers, and hashed credentials; no confirmation of national ID scans or payroll account details at this time.
At 06:10 ICT, we engaged an external forensic advisor under NDA; cross-border data flows from our Singapore development tenant were paused pending legal review of international transfers to ensure PDPA and comparable foreign law alignment.
Per SET Regulation on Disclosure of Information of Listed Companies and SEC Thailand guidance, this constitutes potential inside information; no disclosure has yet been made via SETLink, and the information remains non-public as of the time of this note.
Given the proximity to our blackout window for Q2 results (1–8 August, per our Insider Trading Policy), employees and insiders are reminded to refrain from trading and to avoid selective disclosure to analysts or customers until an official SET News release is posted.
Management’s initial assessment, led by Khun Ratchanee Virojkul (CFO) and Dr. Preecha Nantadilok (CIO), is that direct revenue impact for June is currently estimated at below 1% of consolidated sales; however, potential contract penalty exposure under two enterprise SLAs could render the matter material to investors if service credits exceed THB 28 million.
If materiality is confirmed by 4 June 2026, we will promptly submit a clarification of news report via SETLink within the same business day and provide an MD&A discussion in the next quarterly financial statements filed with the SEC Office; in the interim, inquiries should be directed only to incident@azuratech.co.th.
For precision: the cloud migration roadmap referenced in our Form 56-1 One Report for FY2025 (filed publicly on SET on 28 February 2026) and the 15 March 2026 press release on ESG cybersecurity training remain public, stale information and are unrelated to today’s event.
A third-party notice from CrystalNimbus Cloud (industry-wide service advisory dated 31 May 2026) reported regional latency; that operational update is external and non-issuer-specific, and should not be construed as confirmation of our incident.
We have informed our audit committee chair, Ms. Lalita Chanchai, and initiated internal PDPA breach assessment procedures; if notification thresholds are met, we will notify affected data subjects and the regulator within the statutory timeframe.
All external communications must be centrally coordinated; do not respond to sell-side requests or vendor questionnaires until the SET disclosure is live to avoid any risk of selective disclosure under the Securities and Exchange Act.
If rumors arise or there is unusual trading activity, we will prepare a prompt “Clarification of News” to the SET and consider requesting an H-sign halt only if directed by the exchange following a query.
This document is intended for internal legal and compliance review only and does not constitute the final disclosure; updates will follow after forensic validation and legal materiality determination.
